memoryArtifacts.headline: Will of William Echols, 1771
memoryArtifacts.description:
Halifax County - Will Book O p.310 
WILL OF WILLIAM ECHOLS - Dated: 8 Apr 1771; Prob: 16 May 1771

Lend to Sarah Echols [Sarah TURNER], my wife, the land I now live on plus my corn mill & all my movea …

zh.Halifax, Virginia, British Colonial Amer: 布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州哈利法可
zh.lifeSummary: Sarah Turner於1705年出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州阿梅莉亞。她於大約1720年與William Echols在美國維吉尼亞州結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和7個女兒的父母。她去世於1778年2月1日在美國維吉尼亞州哈利法可享年73歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Sarah Turner于1705年出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州阿梅莉亞。她于大约1720年与William Echols在美国弗吉尼亚州结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和7个女儿的父母。她去世于1778年2月1日在美国弗吉尼亚州哈利法可享年73岁。

nameMeanings.name: Turner
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name from Middle English t(o)urnour, turner ‘turner’ (Old French to(u)rn(e)our), mainly denoting someone who fashioned small objects of wood, metal, or bone on a lathe, but also a variety of other occupations, including turnspit and translator or interpreter. This surname may have become confused with Toner . In North America, it is also very common among African Americans.

English: occasionally perhaps a nickname from Middle English turn-hare, a compound of Middle English tournen ‘to turn, direct, steer’ + hare ‘hare’, a name for someone in charge of the greyhounds in hare coursing or an exaggerated compliment for someone who could run fast. See also Turnbull .
English: perhaps also from Middle English t(o)urn(e)our ‘jouster, one who takes part in a tournament’ (Old French tornoieor, tournoieur).

variants.name: Trinder
meanings: English (Oxfordshire and Gloucestershire): occupational name from a derivative of Middle English trenden ‘to turn, roll, twist’ (Old English trendan), denoting someone who turned something such as wood or fibres. Compare Turner , Spinner .

variants.name: Toner
meanings: Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Tomhrair ‘descendant of Tomhrar’, a Gaelicized form of the Norse personal name Thorr. Compare Thunder .
English: nickname from Old French toner ‘thunder’, perhaps for someone with a thunderous voice. Compare Thunder .

variants.name: Tarner
meanings: English: variant of   Turner  . This form of the surname is relatively rare in Britain and Ireland. It may sometimes have been confused with   Taverner  .
German (North Rhine-Westphalia): unexplained. This surname is rare in Germany.

variants.name: Turney
meanings: habitational name from any of the places in northern France called Tournai-sur-Dive (Orne), Tournay-sur-Odon (Calvados), or Tourny (Eure), or occasionally from Tournai in Hainaut (Belgium).

perhaps occasionally an occupational name from Middle English tournei, turnai ‘lawyer’ (Anglo-Norman French tourné), or a nickname from Middle English tournei(en) ‘(to) joust, take part in a tournament’ (Old French torneier). Compare Turner .

nameMeanings.name: Sarah
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the wife of Abraham and mother of Isaac. According to the Book of Genesis, she was originally called Sarai (possibly meaning ‘contentious’ in Hebrew), but had her name changed by God to the more auspicious Sarah ‘princess’ in token of a greater blessing (Genesis 17:15, ‘And God said unto Abraham, As for Sarai thy wife, thou shalt not call her name Sarai, but Sarah shall her name be’). This has been one of the most enduringly popular girls' names.

variants.name: Sara
meanings: Variant of   Sarah .  This is the form used in the Greek of the New Testament (Hebrews 11:11).

historicEvents.title: Mount Vernon
historicEvents.description: Mount Vernon Plantation was the home of George Washington. It started off as 2,000 acres and was later expanded to 8,000 acres. The house itself started off as a six room building then got extended to twenty-one rooms.
"Patrick Henry made his ""Give me Liberty or Give me Death"" speech in Richmond Virginia."
Thomas Jefferson's American Declaration of Independence endorsed by Congress. Colonies declare independence.

en.lifeSummary: Sarah Turner was born in 1705, in Amelia, Virginia, British Colonial America. She married William Echols about 1720, in Virginia, United States. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 7 daughters. She died on 1 February 1778, in Halifax, Virginia, United States, at the age of 73.

ko.lifeSummary: Sarah Turner(은)는 1705년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 아밀리야에서 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1720년 미국 버지니아 주에서 William Echols(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1778년 2월 1일 미국 버지니아 주 할이파스에서 73세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Sarah Turner nasceu em 1705, em Amelia, Virginia, British Colonial America. Ela casou-se com William Echols aproximadamente 1720, em Virgínia, Estados Unidos da América. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 7 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1 de fevereiro de 1778, em Halifax, Virgínia, Estados Unidos da América, com 73 anos.

ru.Laurens, Laurens, South Carolina, United: лоуренс, лоуренс, Южная Каролина, Соединенные штаты
ru.lifeSummary: Sarah Turner родилась в 1705 г. в местности амеля, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Echols около 1720 г. в местности Вирджиния, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 7 дочерей. Она умерла 1 февраля 1778 г. в местности хэлифакс, Вирджиния, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 73 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Sarah Turner nació en 1705, en Amelia, Virginia, British Colonial America. Se casó con William Echols aproximadamente 1720, en Virginia, Estados Unidos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 7 hijas. Murió el 1 de febrero de 1778, en Halifax, Virginia, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 73 años.
